chief_complaint: A 41-year-old African lady presented with severe abdominal pain and vomiting for three weeks, associated with decreased appetite and weight loss.
history: The patient had no past medical history. She presented with severe abdominal pain and vomiting for three weeks, associated with decreased appetite and weight loss.
exam: No specific physical examination findings were reported.
labs: High anion gap metabolic acidosis, positive ketones in the urine, high serum B-hydroxybutyrate, normal blood glucose readings, normal HbA1c, normal serum lactic acid, and normal salicylate levels. Thyroid functions test (TFT) confirmed primary hyperthyroidism.
correct_diagnosis: Non-diabetic ketoacidosis secondary to primary hyperthyroidism
differentials: Non-diabetic ketoacidosis secondary to primary hyperthyroidism
correct_ranking: Non-diabetic ketoacidosis secondary to primary hyperthyroidism
